# prq

> Developer attention CLI — see what needs you across GitHub and Linear. One command.

prq consolidates PRs, issues, and tickets from GitHub and Linear into a single prioritized queue. Instead of checking GitHub notifications, Linear, and Slack separately, developers run one command to see what needs their attention — categorized by action type: needs re-review, review requested, stale, waiting on others, and mentions.

## Install

npm install -g prq-cli

Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be authenticated.

## Quick Start

# See your review queue (interactive by default)
prq

# Non-interactive / plain text
prq --no-interactive

# Act on a PR
prq review 482
prq open 482
prq nudge 482

## Commands

### prq status (default)

Shows PRs needing your attention in five categories:

- Needs Re-review: new commits pushed after your review
- Requested: you're a requested reviewer
- Stale: no activity for N days (configurable via --stale-days)
- Your PRs Waiting: waiting on someone else
- In Progress: PRs you've marked as started

Options:
  prq                                        # interactive mode (default)
  prq status --repos org/repo1 org/repo2     # specific repos
  prq status --stale-days 7                  # custom threshold
  prq status --filter type:pr                # only PRs
  prq status --filter label:priority         # only items with label
  prq status --filter '!draft:true'          # exclude drafts
  prq status --json                          # machine-readable output
  prq --no-interactive                       # plain text output

### Interactive Mode

Interactive mode is the default when running in a terminal. Keyboard shortcuts:

  Up/Down    Navigate between PRs
  Left/Right Page up / page down
  o          Open — open PR in browser
  n          Nudge — post a comment (won't double-ping)
  s          Start/Stop — mark as in progress
  c          Copy URL to clipboard
  f          Filter — filter by label, author, type, category, repo
  a          Actions — open menu with all actions
  /          Search by number, title, or author
  q          Quit

Press 'f' to open the filter menu. Pick a dimension (label, author, type, etc.), then toggle values. Active filters show a '*' indicator. Press '0' to clear.

Press 'a' to open the actions menu with all actions (built-in and custom). Press 1-9 to run an action, or q to dismiss.

### prq open/review/nudge <identifier>

Act on PRs by number, org/repo#number, or full URL:

  prq open 482                               # open in browser
  prq review 482                             # open files changed
  prq nudge 482                              # post a comment (warns if already nudged)
  prq nudge 482 --yes --message "Update?"    # skip confirmation

### prq run <action> <identifier>

Run any custom action:

  prq run checkout 482
  prq run ai-review 482

### prq skill

Install the /prq skill for Claude Code:

  prq skill            # install in current project
  prq skill --global   # install globally

## Filtering

Filter your queue from the CLI or set defaults in config. Syntax mirrors GitHub's search qualifiers.

  --filter label:priority          # include by label
  --filter '!label:wontfix'        # exclude (prefix with !)
  --filter label:priority,urgent   # OR within a filter
  --filter type:pr --filter author:alice  # AND across filters

Filter keys: label, author, type, category, repo, draft

Set default filters in config:

{
  "filters": ["!draft:true", "!label:wontfix"]
}

CLI --filter flags override config defaults entirely.

## Custom Actions

Every action is a configurable shell command template. Actions run with full terminal control — prq suspends its TUI, the command takes over, and prq resumes when done.

Configure in .prqrc.json:

### Use Claude Code for reviews

{
  "actions": {
    "review": "claude '/review {url}'"
  }
}

### Use Codex for reviews

{
  "actions": {
    "review": "codex exec --full-auto 'review the PR at {url}'"
  }
}

### Use gh CLI to checkout

{
  "actions": {
    "checkout": "gh pr checkout {number} --repo {owner}/{repo}"
  }
}

### Template Variables

  {url}       https://github.com/org/repo/pull/482
  {number}    482
  {owner}     org
  {repo}      repo
  {fullRepo}  org/repo
  {title}     fix: handle edge case
  {author}    alice
  {days}      5
  {category}  needs-re-review
  {target}    @bob, @charlie (reviewers for your PRs, author otherwise)

## Scripting

prq is fully scriptable with --json output and --yes flags:

  # Agent reads the queue
  prq status --json

  # Agent nudges all stale PRs
  prq status --json | jq -r '.prs[] | select(.category == "stale") | .number' \
    | xargs -I{} prq nudge {} --yes

  # Claude Code cron
  prq status --json | claude -p "Review needs-re-review PRs older than 7 days"

## Configuration

Config is loaded in this order (later overrides earlier):

1. ~/.config/prq/config.json — global defaults
2. .prqrc.json — per-project config
3. CLI flags

Full example:

{
  "repos": ["org/repo1", "org/repo2"],
  "staleDays": 5,
  "filters": ["!draft:true", "!label:wontfix"],
  "actions": {
    "review": "claude '/review {url}'",
    "checkout": "gh pr checkout {number} --repo {owner}/{repo}",
    "approve": "gh pr review {number} --repo {owner}/{repo} --approve"
  }
}
